descriptions.description:
This screen shows a web browser displaying the p5.js reference documentation for the `p5.Convolver` and `p5.Delay` classes.

**UI state:**
The application is Google Chrome, with multiple tabs open including "p5.js Web Editor | spectrograph" and "Audio frequency - Wikipedia." The main content area displays documentation for `p5.Convolver` and `p5.Delay`, organized with method names in bold followed by descriptions. A yellow banner at the bottom reads, "Looking for the old p5.js site? Find it here!"

**Visual output:**
The layout is a documentation page with a sidebar on the left containing links like "Reference," "Tutorials," and various p5.js class names (e.g., `p5.sound`, `p5.Amplitude`). The main content area uses a white background with black text, featuring headings for different classes and sub-headings for their respective methods.

The image shows a web browser displaying the documentation page for "p5.sound library" on the `archive.p5js.org` website.

**Visual output:**
The page features a large, pink asterisk-like icon on the left side. The main content area consists of a list of functions and terms related to the p5.sound library, such as `p5.SoundFile`, `p5.Amplitude`, `p5.AudioIn`, and others, each followed by a brief description of its functionality.

**UI state:**
The browser is Google Chrome, with multiple tabs open including "Signals - Miro," "reference | p5.js," and "Audio frequency - Wikipedia." The URL bar shows `archive.p5js.org/reference/#libraries/p5.sound`. The operating system interface (macOS) is visible at the top of the screen, showing a time of 1:55 PM and various system icons.

The image shows a web browser (Chrome) displaying documentation for the `p5.js` library, specifically the `p5.FFT` class within the "Reference" section of the p5.js website.

**Visual output:**
The main content area features a large pink asterisk-like shape on the left and technical documentation text on the right. The text describes the Fast Fourier Transform (FFT) algorithm, explaining how it isolates individual audio frequencies within a waveform. The page includes a sidebar with navigation links such as "Editor," "Download," "Donate," "Get Started," "Reference," "Libraries," "Learn," "Teach," "Examples," "Contribute," "Books," "Community," and "Showcase."

```javascript
52  push()
53  for (let i in octaveAverages) {
54    fill((octaveAverages.length)*255, 50, 10)
55  }
56  pop()
```
